page.title: Settings
page.description: Manage email notifications, event collection and delivery, corporate sign-in (SSO), audit, and licensing.

email.cardDescription: Outgoing mail server (SMTP), sender, and recipients used in alerts and reports.

collector.cardDescription: Adjust batches, rate limits, and concurrency per vendor. Changes take effect automatically within 30 seconds.

identity.cardDescription: Corporate "Sign in with Microsoft" login (OIDC). Credentials are stored securely and changes take effect from the next login.

audit.cardDescription: Latest events sent to destinations (Syslog/JSONL). Use this to check the exact format and test decoders in your destination's tool (e.g., {{tool}}).

capture.cardDescription: Records a temporary sample of traffic sent to destinations, optionally filtered by vendor. Ideal for seeing the real format of events during an integration test. The session expires on its own.

collectorsPage.title: Collectors
collectorsPage.description: Real-time collection state. Each row represents an active collection (integration + data type).
collectorsPage.triggerSuccess: Collection started — new events will appear shortly (queue {{queue}}, id {{taskId}}…).

vendors.collapsedHint: {{count}} vendor streams registered in the collector. Click “View all” to list and filter.

table.title: Collection state
table.sourceHint: Data source: {{table}} — updated on every collection.
table.ariaLabel: Collection state by integration and stream
table.emptyNoVendors: No collection vendor registered yet. Check that all CentralOps services are running — especially the scheduler (beat).
table.emptyNoIntegration: Create an active integration — the first collection starts automatically within 60 seconds.

resetModal.title: Reset cursor?
resetModal.body: This erases the saved read position for {{name}} / {{stream}}.
resetModal.hint: The next collection restarts from the vendor's default point (1 hour back). Recent duplicates are filtered automatically, but some events may be re-sent.

fields.userHelper: Optional. Use when the relay requires authentication.
fields.passwordHelperConfigured: A password is already saved. Fill in only to replace it.
fields.passwordHelperEmpty: Optional. Leave blank if your relay doesn't require a password.

email.useTlsDescription: Enable when the provider requires encrypted transport for SMTP connections.
email.passwordMarkedForRemovalBody: The saved password will be erased when you save the settings.

recipients.title: Recipients
recipients.description: Emails that receive alerts and results from scheduled runs.
recipients.emptyDescription: Add at least one email to receive alert and schedule notifications.
recipients.deleteDescriptionPrefix: Are you sure you want to remove
recipients.deleteDescriptionSuffix: ? This email will stop receiving alerts and results from scheduled runs.

envBanner.title: Configuration set during installation
envBanner.body: The current values come from the installation's {{envFile}} file. Save to start editing them here — changes take effect automatically within 30 seconds.

collector.destinationsCta: The delivery destination configuration (e.g., wazuh-default) has moved to the Destinations page.

bufferDedupe.title: Buffer & Dedupe
bufferDedupe.hint: How batches are accumulated and duplicate events are filtered.

domainConcurrency.title: Concurrency per domain
domainConcurrency.hint: Maximum simultaneous requests to each vendor's host.

vendorRateLimits.title: Rate limits per vendor
vendorRateLimits.hint: Maximum requests per second, minute, and hour for each vendor.

fields.batchSizeHelper: Larger batches make fewer sends, but each event may take longer to arrive.
fields.flushSecondsHelper: Sends the batch even if incomplete after this time — sets the maximum wait time for an event.
fields.dedupeTtlHelper: Memory window for filtering duplicate events per vendor.

table.selectVendorRateLimitAriaLabel: Select vendor to add rate limit

identity.enableEntraLoginDescription: Shows the 'Sign in with Microsoft' button on the login screen.

fields.redirectUriHelper: Must match what's registered in the App Registration (HTTPS).
fields.authorityHelper: Only change for sovereign clouds (US Gov / China).
fields.defaultRoleHelper: Used when no App Role in the token matches the map.
fields.allowedEmailDomainsHelper: Separate with commas. Empty = any domain in the tenant.

identity.globalScopeDefaultDescription: Accounts created via SSO see all organizations (typical case for an internal SOC).
identity.jitProvisioningDescription: Creates the account on first login. Turn off to require a pre-provisioned account.

sync.title: User Sync (Graph)
sync.enabledDescription: Enables periodic sync of App Registration users via Microsoft Graph.
sync.deprovisionDescription: Deactivate users who leave the App Registration (sessions and tokens revoked).

identity.envOnlyHint: Values come from the installation's configuration — save to edit them here.

status.partialAriaLabel: Last sync completed with partial errors

entraSync.cardDescription: Latest Entra user sync via Microsoft Graph API.
entraSync.statusRegionAriaLabel: Status of the latest Entra user sync

intro.title: What this tab is
intro.body: Rolling sample of the latest events actually sent to destinations (up to 500, kept for 24h). The wire-format lines exported here are byte-for-byte identical to what went out on the network, including hostname and priority. Paste into your destination's test tool (e.g., {{tool}}) to see exactly how your decoder or rule behaves with real traffic.

audit.copyWireSuccess: {{format}} line copied — paste it into your destination's test tool (e.g., {{tool}}) to test decoders.
audit.emptyDescription: No events sent in the last few minutes — or you just cleared the sample. It fills up again on the next collection.

table.legacyFormatTooltip: Sample recorded before a system update — actual format not recorded

actions.wireTooltip: Copy wire-format line for your destination's test tool

modal.wireLineCopyHint: (copy to your destination's test tool, e.g., {{tool}})
modal.legacyEnvelopeNote: Event recorded by an earlier version of the system: the hostname shown is generic, but this doesn't affect the decoder — identification uses {{field}}. New events carry the real hostname.
modal.legacyFormatNote: Sample recorded before a system update — displayed in RFC 5424 format.

clearDialog.title: Clear the audit sample?
clearDialog.descriptionSuffix: currently in the buffer and cannot be undone. New events will keep being recorded normally as collectors dispatch to destinations.

intro.title: What live capture is
intro.body: Turns on a temporary "listening mode" that records a sample of the traffic processed by the pipeline along with each event's outcome (delivered, dropped, no destination, quarantined…), optionally filtered by vendor. Use it to see how a log came in and how it went out during an integration test — without searching the general audit. The session expires on its own at the end of the window; nothing stays on forever.

capture.startSuccess: Capture started{{vendorSuffix}}. Events appear as they are sent to destinations.
capture.limitReached: Simultaneous capture session limit reached. Stop or delete an existing session before starting another.
capture.stopSuccess: Capture stopped. Already-recorded events remain available.

form.orgRequiredHint: Select an organization to capture its traffic.
form.durationHint: Collectors run in cycles (from 1 to a few minutes). A short window can open and close between two cycles and capture nothing — for troubleshooting, prefer 15 minutes or more.

capture.sessionsEmptyDescription: Start a capture above to record a sample of dispatched traffic. The session expires on its own at the end of the chosen window.

events.title: Events — {{vendor}}
events.waitingDescription: The capture is live and hasn't recorded anything yet. That usually means no traffic has gone through the pipeline in this window — not that the capture is broken.
events.emptyDescription: The window ended with no events. Either there was no traffic in that period, or it didn't match the chosen filter.
events.filteredEmptyDescription: The session captured {{total}} event(s), but none with that outcome. Clear the filter to see them all.
events.waitingWhyPipeline: • The capture records what the pipeline processes, with each event's outcome.
events.waitingWhyCadence: • Collectors run in cycles: it can take 1 to a few minutes until the first batch.
events.waitingWindow: • This session's window: ~{{minutes}} min. If it's shorter than the collector cadence, it may close without catching anything.
events.waitingVendorFilter: • Scoped to vendor “{{vendor}}”: traffic from other vendors won't enter this session.
events.waitingServerCounts: The server already counted {{total}} event(s) in this session — refresh to load them.
events.emptyRetryHint: • Tip: retry with a longer window (15 min or more) and no vendor filter to confirm.

deleteDialog.title: Delete capture session?
deleteDialog.description: Removes the session and all events captured in it. Cannot be undone.

outcomes.unknownTooltip: Event recorded before the capture started tracking outcomes.

licensing.cardDescription: Activate the Enterprise edition by pasting the license key. It's validated offline, with no external connection, and stays active across updates and restarts — no additional configuration needed.
licensing.graceBody: Enterprise features remain temporarily active. Paste the renewed license below before the grace period ends to avoid reverting to Community.
licensing.activateSuccessCommunityOnly: License accepted, but the system remains on the Community edition. The license may have been revoked — contact whoever issued it.

form.label: License key
form.helper: Digitally signed key issued for your organization. Validated offline.

edition.graceBody: The Enterprise license expired, but features remain active during the grace period. Renew now — once the grace period ends, the system returns to Community mode.
edition.expiredBody: The Enterprise license expired and the system returned to Community mode. Renew the license to reactivate your plan's features.
edition.expiringSoonBody: Your license expires in {{days}} day(s) ({{date}}). Renew to avoid losing your plan's features — the system reverts to Community once it expires.

panel.title: Backfill — controlled historical collection
panel.description: Recover events from past periods without affecting real-time collection.

table.emptyDescription: Use the 'New backfill' button to start a controlled historical collection.

cancelDialog.title: Cancel backfill
cancelDialog.description: Are you sure you want to cancel job {{jobId}}? This action cannot be undone.

form.info: The backfill runs separately from real-time collection, without affecting it. Historical events get the same processing and delivery as everything else. Maximum window: {{maxDays}} days.
form.errorDateOrder: The start date must be earlier than the end date.
form.windowExceededBody: Select a period of {{maxDays}} days or less to avoid overloading the system.
form.successBody: The backfill has been queued and will start shortly.
